pi_items.source: Downloaded and verified from https://wiki.wireshark.org/uploads/__moin_import__/attachments/SampleCaptures/smtp.pcap (28 KB file)
Task description specifies saving to ~/Documents/cisco_block_rule.txt; Wireshark Firewall ACL Rules tool generates syntax: 'access-list NUMBER deny host <IP>'
Task description specifies saving to ~/Documents/iptables_block_rule.txt; Wireshark Firewall ACL Rules tool generates syntax: 'iptables -A INPUT -s <IP>/32 -j DROP'
tshark analysis of smtp.pcap: 'tshark -r smtp.pcap -Y "smtp.req.command" -T fields -e ip.src' returns 10.10.1.4 as sender of EHLO command
tshark analysis of smtp.pcap reveals SMTP server responding to client EHLO from 74.53.140.153 (xc90.websitewelcome.com)

privileged_info_summary: The task uses smtp.pcap, an official Wireshark sample PCAP file (28 KB) containing synthetic SMTP traffic. The file is downloaded from Wireshark's official sample captures repository. The client initiating SMTP traffic (sending EHLO) has IP address 10.10.1.4, which is the IP that should be blocked by both Cisco IOS and iptables rules. Wireshark's Firewall ACL Rules tool generates: Cisco IOS format as 'access-list NUMBER deny host <IP>' and iptables format as 'iptables -A INPUT -s <IP>/32 -j DROP'. The expected output files are cisco_block_rule.txt and iptables_block_rule.txt respectively.
